title: Detect Suspicious Commands Initiated by Webserver Processes
description:
This query was originally published in the threat analytics report, Operation Soft Cell.
Operation Soft Cell is a series of campaigns targeting users' call logs at telecommunications providers throughout the world. These attacks date from as early as 2012.
Operation Soft Cell operators sometimes use legitimate web server processes to launch commands, especially for network discovery and user/owner discovery. The following query detects activity of this kind.
Reference - https://www.cybereason.com/blog/operation-soft-cell-a-worldwide-campaign-against-telecommunications-providers
query: ('Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Security Events') and (((('Parent Process Name' in ('w3wp.exe', 'beasvc.exe', 'httpd.exe') or 'Parent Process Name' like 'tomcat*') or 'Parent Process Name' in ('w3wp.exe', 'beasvc.exe', 'httpd.exe') or 'Parent Process Name' like 'tomcat*')) and 'Process Name' in ('cmd.exe', 'powershell.exe')) and ('Command Line' like '*%temp%*' or 'Command Line' like '*wget*' or 'Command Line' like '*whoami*' or 'Command Line' like '*certutil*' or 'Command Line' like '*systeminfo*' or 'Command Line' like '*ping*' or 'Command Line' like '*ipconfig*' or 'Command Line' like '*timeout*') | stats unique('Process Name') as any_FileName, unique('Command Line') as set_ProcessCommandLine, unique('Parent Process Name') as any_InitiatingProcessFileName, unique('Parent Process Name') as any_InitiatingProcessParentFileName by Entity, 'Host Name (Server)'

ask_ai_prompts: Summarize the Microsoft Sentinel converted detection results and identify the highest-risk pivots.
